item.title: Here are 20 dog gifs that I hope brighten your Wednesday
item.description:
Welcome to the 231st installment in this series Here are 20 dog gifs that I hope brighten your Monday https://imgur.com/gallery/AE8TIbF Day 221-230 Here are 20 dog gifs that I hope brighten your day https://imgur.com/a/B81rJcL Days 211-220 Here are 20 dog gifs that I hope brighten your day https://imgur.com/a/lwhZE43 Days 201-210 Here are 20 dog gifs that I hope brighten your day https://imgur.com/a/fBko2Pg Fourth 50 links - Here are 20 dog gifs that I hope brighten your day https://imgur.com/a/Jtm8Lqz Third 50 links - Here are 20 dog gifs that I hope brighten your day https://imgur.com/a/XvE00DP Second 50 links - Here are 20 dog gifs that I hope brighten your day https://imgur.com/a/ewb2izC First 50 links - Here are 20 dog gifs that I hope brighten your day https://imgur.com/a/iqtNz8u
It's Nanna Pants ＼(°o°)／
MrsPuckett puts up with a lot
MrsPuckett puts up with a lot part 2
PJ does this every single night
MrsPuckett puts up with a lot part 3
Always gotta steal the kisses
My 6 year old, 2 year old and 16 year old puppies The Pucketts ... I'm doing a fundraiser to help the Humane Association continue rescuing dogs like Nanna. I'd love it if you checked out my link below https://www.classy.org/fundraiser/3793511 ... Have a beautiful day everyone, see you soon
item.dc:creator: MrPuckett

item.title: Can Imgur help me in remembering my brother?
item.description:
I lost my brother last weekend... He was severely disabled with epilepsy, but he was always happy and laughing. His condition was chronic so over the years he lost the ability to walk and talk. And yet he was always full of joy; grinning and laughing when you would talk to him. He would smack things with his hand as a way of interacting with his surroundings so we would have lots of fun doing high-fives.
Its hard looking back on all the memories, but I know its really important to process through the grief. I know Imgur is a big mix of lots of beliefs and backgrounds; my family is Christian and it helps to picture him in Heaven in a healed and healthy body. When he was in hospice, I kept imagining him being able to run through green fields and play and laugh. The Bible talks about the Earth being redeemed and being made perfect again (we can all agree things are pretty messed up now, right) so its nice to think about him enjoying Heaven by doing all the things he wasn't able to in this life.
There are so many talented artists on here so I was hoping I could get some help with making a memorial picture/painting of him. I have this image of him laughing and running through green, grassy fields. Would any artistic imgurians be willing to help me make a painting that I can remember him by? I have included some pictures from his life that I hope will help. If more would be useful, I am currently going through our old photos and making digital copies. Hopefully, I will have more soon. Thanks in advance
See you on the other side Mathew!
item.dc:creator: chillychiweenie

item.title: I made a blanket fort that's 30% TV screen
item.description: I'm 24 and I have work tomorrow. But, no one yelled at me. No one called me stupid. No one told me to stop being a baby. No one told me I'm wasting my time. No one told me I was making a mess. No one told me I'm just wasting space. And I had the space for it, it didn't get too overwhelming to make, I didn't have to throw away 3 bags of trash to make space for it, I didn't have to beg my partner to help, I didn't stress about what I was doing, I didn't worry about not having the materials I wanted to do it right This might all seem so silly but to me it's huge. I grew up in a family telling me everything I was doing was wrong, then had a depressed fiance while also depressed myself, so out apartment was constantly filthy and you couldn't see the floor I've completely turned my life around in the past 7 months, I'm single and free and just... Man I'm just happy. All the stuffed animals have names, some are obvious and some aren't, but they all wanted to come join me in my blanket fort The rest of the living room is a mess but no where near as bad as it used to be, and the space in general just feels bigger I've wanted to make a blanket fort like this my entire life, I think it's going to stick around for a while TL:DR, I have a blanket fort and you don't. Edit: all vents are clear! Everything has been carefully constructed to make sure there's no hazards! I have no appropriate snacks cause I'm mega broke but this will be sticking around for a while! Second edit: I'm also gonna sneakily place my PayPal riiiiight here for snack funds @ThaliaWho
item.dc:creator: ThaliaWho

item.dc:creator: BlastFemur

item.title: Told My Colleague My Hobbies Was Dressmaking, She Responded With A Condescending "Cringe"
item.description: I'm done. I'm tired of being judged on a hobby I genuinely enjoyed because of the way I look. I can't control how the fact I'm a large burly man. Every woman I talk to about it in person just treats me like I'm a creep, every guy treats me like I'm a pussy. After work I'm packing my sewing equipment away. No more tailoring, no more dresses, no more cosplay, no more adding pockets to shit. No more. If anyone asks my hobbies are weightlifting, deiting, and screaming into the void. Edit: I gave away the dresses I made. I did this for skill and to make the world a prettier place, not internet points and clearly not to get laid. All I have left is my very shitty first attempt at making a suit that I kept as a reminder of how far I'd come. Not far enough apparently.
item.dc:creator: Beattywill80

item.title: Just digging in a graveyard - an ordinary thing that ordinary people do…
item.description:
Recap: I clear out overgrown graves in my local cemetery. Link: https://imgur.com/gallery/oJCrtWq . It’s been a week since I’ve last posted. Sorry about that. I got caught up in some things. I’ve been trying to work diligently to get these overgrown gravestones cleared out. . Small shout out to Lisa, a gravestone restoration expert who has taken interest in my posts! I hope to be able to get more of your wisdom later on!
I don’t do anything to the gravestones themselves, aside from washing fresh mud off of them. I am trained by the University of Texas Horticulture Department to do “garden restoration.” . How to clean a grave: DON’T. Here’s why: https://imgur.com/gallery/S8QxYZk . Now, before I get into the grave-clearing, I want to give Imgur a special thank you.
Some of you were so kind as to donate supplies and money to help me out with this project. Link: https://imgur.com/gallery/T7iaA6E I wanted to say thank you. I mean it, truly. All of your donations have made my project 1000000x easier!
Voila! Unfortunately, I wasn’t able to do any more graves…
April showers bring May flowers, and ants - which brings a shower of tears down a certain nerd’s face. (I hope that was a clever play on words and not totally lame lol)
This isn’t a grave I worked on. It was cleared out pretty well, and didn’t need anything that I could have done for it. However, I’ve been having a hell of a time trying to film the ants. This grave was easier to show the anthills, and this is exactly the same as every other grave that I HAVE been trying to work on. . . My biggest concern is that I don’t know what kind of ants these are. I come from Texas, which is the land of fire ants. Fire ants aren’t just insects that leave a painful bite - it has venom in it. This venom hurts like hell. While uncommon, fire ants can cause serious health problems - depending on the number of bites, or if you have an allergic reaction to them. An analogy: A single wasp sting doesn’t kill a person (unless they have an allergic reaction), but getting stung by an entire wasp nest could very well kill them. This is the same with fire ants - albeit this is less common. . Since I do not know the type of ants that have made these gravestones their abode, I didn’t want to fuck with them. However, this is secondary. In theory, I could do something about the ants so they aren’t a problem for me. Except I can’t do that.
Here’s why the ants are actually putting a stop to my project. I have a very strict gravestone policy: WHATEVER I DO CAN EASILY BE UNDONE. Ant poison is too risky for my comfort. Certain bug-killers can actually kill a garden faster than an unwanted insect/animal can. Whatever I did with ant poison could not be undone (at least not easily undone). If I’m not 100% confident in what I’m doing, I’m not going to do it with a grave. I will sometimes take risks in other areas, but NOT this one.
I’m not joking. I inspected EIGHT other graves, and they ALL had ants! :(
Should I get protective gear? Is there protective gear out there for ants? An ant hazmat suit? All advice is appreciated!
I feel bad that this post is kinda boring. I decided to show you some other things I’ve found in this graveyard, as well as my decision-making when choosing a gravestone to work on. I have a process of deciding which graves I will work on, and which ones I will leave as is. I decided to include a clip of a few graves I chose to leave as is, and why.
I came across this curious symbol, and wanted to share it with Imgur to see if anyone recognized it? I have no idea what it is. Thanks for your help!
Oftentimes when I make a meme of an image **I** took, it gets blurry. Here’s what it says, in case it’s too blurry to read: 104th U.S. INF.
I don’t fix gravestones, only gardens. I didn’t want to try to prop it up, only for it to fall over again and sustain further damage. I’ll report this one to the graveyard as well.
I don’t handle every overgrown grave I see. I have a selection process. I thought y’all might like to see it. The following are a few graves that I chose not to handle and why.
I’m going to report this one to the grave keeper. I’m worried that this grave fell face-down. That could have done some damage to the engraving. Why don’t I try to move it? A gravestone has a base that sometimes breaks off (like now). If I tried to pick this up, it would immediately fall over again. The more it fell, the more damage it would sustain. It’s like constantly dropping an unprotected iphone. The more you drop it, the bigger the cracks will become. Some of the screen might even chip off. This is especially the case with old gravestones. It is much safer for me to leave it as is rather than messing with it.
At some point, a gravestone becomes a matter of historical preservation that goes beyond garden restoration. No matter how gentle I am, anything that I could do to this grave could put it in danger of sustaining damages. Think of this like a book. A newer book can be taken care of by proper storage and keeping the dust off of it. The older the book gets, the more delicate it becomes. The pages get stiffer, they crinkle, and they run a chance of tearing. The ink is seriously fading. The binding is falling apart, and the cover is beginning to break. A 100 year old book needs more care than a 20 year old book. I don’t have grave-restoration training. I’m not touching a century old grave (or close to a century old). You could not pay me to do it.
Sometimes I decide to leave a grave alone, simply due to its placement in the graveyard. If it’s on the edge of a slope, I’m leaving it alone. It’s just too risky. I also won’t do graves where the headstones are half-sunken in. At that point, it needs a grave-restoration professional to lift up and return to its proper position.
I hope this was interesting, and not a disorganized mess!
There are many understandable concerns about my project. These are questions that have come up on every single graveyard post. I’m not bitter about this at all. I’m happy to know that you care about these headstones as much as I do. Some of these are things I’ve said before, but I wanted to say it again so it’s on the record. I hope this puts some concerns to rest!
item.dc:creator: OlivetheNerd

item.title: It's like he's a completely different cat
item.description: Some of you may remember my post from yesterday about how the new cat I got kept hiding behind the toilet. https://imgur.com/gallery/EpioPeT Well I'm happy to say the toilet gremlin is no more! He was ready to explore and has very quickly adjusted to his new home. I can't believe it! He changed practically overnight. Thank you to everyone everyone who gave advice!
item.dc:creator: WaffleBrat
